resources.string: Choose an option from the menu.
Do you want to save changes?

Where do you have stored or want to store your ROM files?

By default, an empty internal folder will be used (but you should manually copy your ROMs files there using a PC). You can also select a folder with ROMs files from your external storage which will need to be authorized in the next step so MAME4droid can read the ROMS from it.

Also, if you select an external storage folder, your ROMs files will not be deleted when the app is uninstalled and you can use an Android file manager to move your ROMs there without needing a PC.
Your device doesn\'t have the native android file manager needed to authorize external storage reading.

\n\nSome Android TV devices don\'t include the OS document picker which is needed to grant folder permissions for the apps on Android 11+. As an alternative, select default which use the App Media Folder which is supported on all devices and is created at Android/media/[app package name]. Move any emulator files into that folder with a file manager so the app can access them without any special permissions.
Are you sure you want to exit from app?
MAME4droid needs to restart for the changes to take effect.
You don\'t have permission to read from external storage. Please, allow storage permission on Android applications settings.
Enter peer IP address (ip or ip:port):
Or paste the shared invite: the best address is picked automatically.
Optional: only needed if the connection doesn\'t establish by itself. Paste the peer\'s shared message (their public IP).
Local network game: connecting directly on your Wi-Fi / network.\n\nMake sure both devices are on the same network and use the same port.
Internet play: if the other player can\'t reach your public IP, tap Peer IP and enter THEIR public address, or forward UDP %1$d on your router.
Router port mapped (UPnP): peers can join your public IP directly.
Mobile data only: no local network (internet play only)
Tap Share to send these addresses via any messenger.
Your public IPv6 (internet or same network): %1$s
Your public IPv6 (internet play, via MOBILE DATA): %1$s
No public IPv6: internet play isn\'t possible, but same-network play works:
No usable IPv6 on this device. Set Network protocol to IPv4 or Auto to play.
The address is %1$s but the Network protocol setting is %2$s. Paste a matching address or change the setting.
Tip: mobile networks usually have IPv6 — try setting Network protocol to IPv6 or Auto on BOTH devices.
Tip: try setting Network protocol to Auto on BOTH devices — if the networks have IPv6, it bypasses this NAT.
Symmetric NAT detected. Internet play may fail.
Your public IP: unavailable (internet play may need port forwarding)
Same public IP as the host: you seem to be on their network -- join their LOCAL IP instead.
If the connection won\'t land: tap Share and send this address to the host (their Peer IP button).
NetPlay: Peer is paused, please wait...
NetPlay: incompatible app builds (local v%1$s / peer v%2$s). Update BOTH devices to the same version.
NetPlay: desync detected -- this game may not be Rollback-safe. Try Resync, or switch to Lockstep.
NetPlay: initial state sync failed (state too large: %1$s MB). Session may desync.
NetPlay: host is running \'%1$s\', loading it.
NetPlay: peer requested a resync, syncing game state...
NetPlay: this game\'s savestate is not rollback-compatible across devices. Using lockstep mode.
NetPlay: initial sync timed out. Resuming offline.
NetPlay: savestate too large for rollback (%1$s MB). Using lockstep mode.
NetPlay: Connection lost. Resuming offline play...
NetPlay: Peer disconnected. Resuming offline play...
Deleted MAME cfg and NVRAM files...

Created or updated: '%1$s' to store save states, cfg files and MAME assets.

Note, copy or move your zipped ROMs under '%2$s' directory!

IMPORTANT: MAME4droid (Current) uses only %3$s MAME romset, not 0.139.

Welcome to MAME4droid for META Quest!

You should pair an XBOX One controller on your META Quest to be able to play games but you can play lightgun games with your touch controllers.
Control layout customization is only allowed in fullscreen mode
Control layout customization is only allowed when touch controller is visible

When MAME4droid is first run, it will create a folder structure for you on the internal memory of your Android device. This folder contains all the other folders MAME uses as well as some basic configuration files.Since MAME4droid does not come with game ROM files, you will need to copy them to (You can change it in options to external storage if your device allows it) '/storage/emulated/0/Android/media/com.seleuco.mame4d2024/roms' folder (the one that applies) yourself. These should be properly named, ZIPped MAME v0.272 ROMs files with the filenames in all lower case.

Important: You should define or map your Android TV game controller on 'options/settings/input/External controller/define Keys' to avoid this help screen constantly showing if the controller is not auto detected.

Controls: Buttons A,B,C,D,E,F on the controller map to buttons Button MAME 1 to 6 buttons. Coin button inserts coin/adds credit.START button starts 1P game. A+START is fast forward. B+START is toggle UI controls. START+A+B is service mode. SELECT+A+B is soft reset. Use SELECT(coin) or START for UI navigation.R1 + START loads a save state. L1 + START saves a save state. START + SELECT when gaming accesses the game's MAME menu (dip switches, etc)...
Media scraping stopped (there is not enough free space)
Media scraping ends (you should restart MAME4droid...)
Error creating effect shader... reverting to stock!
WARNING: Hardware rejected HDR 16-bit. Falling back to SDR.
Detected controller as %1$s\n(Defaults applied) Please map the buttons in Settings

MAME4droid doesn\'t have permission to read the roms files on %1$s.\n\nPlease grant permissions again or select another ROMs folder, both in MAME4droid menu \'Options -> Settings -> General -> Change ROMs path\'.
The ROMs folder has changed since it was cached.\nRestarting MAME4droid is recommended.
Reuse the cached ROMs folder index at startup for faster loading. Disable to fully rescan the folder on every launch.
Force a full rebuild of the ROMs folder cache on the next start (the option switches itself off afterwards).
Also on normal startup, resolve SD files on demand instead of scanning or loading the whole index. Fastest boot; file lists are read as needed.
Are you sure to restore? This will remove all your MAME cfg and nvram files. This is useful to restore games to defaults to fixup mame key mappings.
Are you sure to restore CRT settings to factory defaults?
Are you sure to restore HDR settings to defaults?
Use the Unicode font (unifont) for MAME\'s UI instead of its built-in font. Chinese, Japanese and Korean always use it.
Show MAME\'s built-in menus and messages in the selected language instead of English.
Select it to open emulation options
Enable it to get better performance in games like K.Instinct, M.Kombat or CPS3. You should only enable it to play those games because other games may work poorly or not work.
Enable it to turn FPS display on
Enable it to turn auto frameskip on
Enables the reading of the cheat database
Enable it to activate hiscore MAME plugin to save and restores high scores for games that did not originally save high scores in non-volatile memory
Enable it to allows you to trigger a sequence of emulated input actions with a key or button combination. Note: The plugin is available from MAME UI plugin options
Enable it to allows you to simulate repeatedly pressing an emulated button by holding down a key or button combination. Note: The plugin is available from MAME UI plugin options
Forces to skip displaying the system info screen

Automatically zooms the emulated display so that portrait and landscape games use the entire device window. Disable if you use artwork to zoom into the screen area in MAME video options using fixed orientation and wide resolutions
Disable DRC (dynamic recompiler) CPU core. Enable it to test compatibility in some games that freezes or not launch at the expense of performance
Force DRC to use the C backend (compatibility but much less performance)
Select it to open vector games options
Sets the width of the vectors to 2x
Restore MAME data to default values (mame.ini, mame_avail.ini, cfg and nvram files)
Select it to open input options
Select it to open touch controller options
Select to customize fullscreen touch control layout
Restore fullscreen touch control layout to default values
Enable to hide stick or dpad when fullscreen
Enable it to always show G,H buttons when full screen so you can bind them MAME actions
Animated touch control. Disable for performance boost
Select it to open external controller options
Automatically detect and map known gamepads (e.g., Xbox or PlayStation).
Assign your physical controller or keyboard to the arcade D-pad and buttons.
Reset all button configurations to their original default values.

Forces all controllers to share the exact same mapping profile. Map the first one controller profile, and the rest will use the same layout (Multiplayer is still supported). Ideal for identical clone gamepads or hardware with detection issues.
Hide touch controls in MAME UI
Hide the on-screen buttons in the main interface when a controller is connected (fullscreen only).
Hide the on-screen buttons during gameplay when a controller is connected (fullscreen only).
Select it to open mouse options.\n(On touch mouse, second finger will be main fire. Third finger when second finger is pressed will be alternate fire)
Enable to use touch mouse for games (autodetected).
Forces the use of the touch mouse in all games (useful when fails autodetect).
Select it to open keyboard options
Enable to auto hide touch controller when physical keyboard is detected
Select it to open touch lightgun options.\n(First finger will be main fire. Second finger when first finger is pressed will be alternate fire)
Enable to use touch lightgun for games (autodetected).
Forces the use of the touch lighgun in all games (useful when fails autodetect).
Enable to use first finger long press as button two. Useful for t.crisis
Touch at the bottom of the screen to get off-screen reload
Select it to open tilt controller options
Enable to use tilt sensor for games.
Enable to use tilt sensor as analog device, disable to use as digital
Enable to tap anywhere on screen to fire. First finger is main fire. Second finger when first finger is pressed is alternate fire
Enable to swap Z,Y axes. This fixes vertical tilt on some devices
Enable support for navigating MAME menus / arworks using the touch screen
Override MAME deadzone and saturation so touch stick and lightgun are accurate
Allow a single input device to control multiple players at once. Could cause problems in some ROMs

Enables MAME\'s native renderer instead of the legacy (well tested) software renderer. Unlocks shaders and improves vector games. Important: Set Emulated Resolution to \'Native\' to enjoy high-res artworks without any performance drop.
Select to turn bitmap filtering on
Configure True HDR for vector games and Auto HDR for raster games.

Unlocks peak panel brightness and Wide Color Gamut. Note: HDR experience will vary across devices due to poor implementations by many manufacturers (optimized for Google Pixel). Enabling this renders vector games in true HDR, realistically emulating the intense brightness of original CRT phosphors. It also unlocks Auto HDR enhancements for classic raster games.
The physical baseline light of a standard vector (Default: 250 nits).
The absolute physical limit of the emulated arcade monitor (Default: 700 nits).
Darkens bezels and backgrounds in vector games to maximize the spectacular contrast of the phosphor lasers.
Sets the baseline physical brightness strictly for standard games and artworks in HDR mode. Independent from Vector Nits. Adjust for visual comfort if it appears too bright.
Upgrades standard SDR games and artworks to HDR luminance levels using Inverse Tone Mapping.
How aggressively highlights are expanded to peak luminance.
Reset all HDR and Auto HDR calibration values to defaults.
Enables a highly accurate physical simulation of vector CRT monitors. Includes optical bloom, phosphor persistence, electron beam dynamics, corner burn, and magnetic jitter.
Fine-tune optical bloom, exposure, electron beam physics and yoke voltage.

Renders vector passes at 50% resolution.\n• GAIN: Saves massive GPU fillrate and battery, making the glow smoother.\n• LOSS: Sacrifices vector core crispness, introducing jagged edges (aliasing) on sharp geometry like text and ships.

Converts the raw electrical voltage into physical light energy using the CRT\'s natural Gamma curve (~2.5). Essential for accurate color mixing, though some games may appear less vivid as a result. Highly recommended when True HDR Display is enabled.
Calculate light scattering inside the CRT glass (Halos).
Expands the blur radius of the tent filter.
Minimum energy required for a vector to emit a glowing halo.
Final opacity multiplier of the light halo.
Simulates the whole CRT glass glowing when intense laser beams (like explosions in starwars) bounce off the interior walls of the monitor.
Multiplier for the off-screen energy. Turn it down if text or menus flash too brightly.
Physical thickness of the central laser core for drawing lines.
Physical thickness of the central laser core for isolated dots.
Multiplies the light energy specifically for dots/stars to make them pop.
Calculates physical expansion when the vector exceeds peak brightness.
Absolute limit for extra HDR energy a vector can accumulate.
How much lines widen when saturated with excess energy.
How much points widen when saturated with excess energy.
How much excess energy bleeds into other channels to create white highlights.
Simulates the beam burning harder on short strokes (like text and ships).
Extra light energy given to tiny strokes.
How much the core physically widens when burning the phosphor harder.
Simulates the beam slowing down at sharp corners, causing bright weld points.
How sharp an angle must be to trigger deceleration and burning.
Extra energy dumped into the corner weld.
Restricts the corner burn to keep it sharp and avoid spherical blobs.
Simulates how green colors excite the phosphor more efficiently than blue.
Minimum energy retained by inefficient colors like pure blue.
How much the specific color boosts the physical energy output.
Lower = Long Radar smear. Higher = Fast Arcade fade.
Simulate coil heat, AC hum, and voltage fluctuations.
Maximum physical deviation of the electron beam.
Maximum intensity drop caused by random voltage fluctuation.
Camera eye-adaptation. Dims the screen dynamically during bright explosions.
Used only if Auto-Exposure is OFF. Adjusts the baseline brightness.
Boosts or dims the overall dynamic range of the adaptation.
Screen area percentage that triggers maximum dimming (radar/explosions).
Reset all advanced CRT calibration values to factory defaults.
Select it to configure media scraping service

Enable it to do media scraping thanks to Arcade Database by motoschifo. This will download icons or snapshots belonging to your own ROMSET so that they will be displayed on the frontend. You must configure the display of images in MAME UI (in the \'show side panels\' option) if is necessary. Note: Media scraping will only scrape systems on external storage
Enable it to scrape icons that will be showed on the system list
Enable it to scrape snapshots that will be showed on the images panel
Enable to scrape the rest of available media like cabinets images. This will use up a lot of storage space and the download will take longer
Enable it to resize images so that less storage space is used
Select it to open general options
Change roms location. You have to move ROMs files manually
Game states will be stored under ROM files folder. This prevents Android 11+ from deleting them when using external storage for ROMs files and uninstalling the app
Enable it to warn when you press the exit button when gaming
Expand display area to use notch area on devices supported
Use the MAME simple UI instead of the cabinet one. Simple UI will show you 20 random games but you can search for any by typing with the keyboard
Enable it to scale beyond screen boundaries when a fixed scale is selected
Enable it to apply a 10 pct safe area to correct overscan issues on some TV sets
Select it to open NetPlay options
Small ping/delay indicator at the bottom of the screen during a NetPlay session
Detect and warn about NetPlay desyncs (Rollback only); disable to save the extra CPU cost
When hosting, automatically forward the netplay port on the router so internet peers can join directly. Removed on disconnect
IPv4, IPv6 or automatic. IPv6 has no NAT: internet play usually connects directly, with no port forwarding and fewer connection problems. Auto prefers IPv6 and falls back to IPv4
Select it to open advanced options

Use mame.ini instead of the values established by the emulator automatically to define the paths of the ROMs and other resources in external storage. Enabling this option can prevent you from seeing your ROMs if you don\'t know what you are doing
Disable to hide the touch controller. Useful when you are using a GamePad
Select to enable fullscreen portrait mode
